zh.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: 英國斯塔福爾黑阿德爾
zh.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco於1175年出生在英國斯塔福爾黑阿德爾。他與Unknown of Cheadle結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1225年享年50歲。

zh-hans.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: 英国斯塔福爾黑阿德爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco于1175年出生在英国斯塔福爾黑阿德爾。他与Unknown of Cheadle结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1225年享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bosco
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Carmine, Sal, Ignazio, Antonio, Attilio, Cosimo, Francesco, Gaspare, Luigi, Saverio.

Italian: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from bosco ‘wood’, from Late Latin boscus ‘shrub, undergrowth’ (of Gallic or ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from a place called with this word. In some cases it may have been an occupational name for a woodsman or forester and, by extension, a nickname for a surly or rough person. Compare Lo Bosco .

variants.name: Boschetti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelina, Antonio, Guido, Rosario.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Bosco  .

variants.name: Lo Bosco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Gennaro, Sal, Salvatore, Antonino, Nichola, Pasquale, Piera, Raffaela, Rocco.
Italian (southern; also Lobosco): variant of Bosco , with the addition of the definite article lo.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco was born in 1175, in Cheadle, Staffordshire, England. He married Unknown of Cheadle. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1225, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco est né en 1175, à Cheadle, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Il a épousé Unknown of Cheadle. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1225, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco wurde 1175 in Cheadle, Staffordshire, England geboren. Er hat Unknown of Cheadle geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1225 im Alter von 50 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco è nato il nel 1175 nel Cheadle, Staffordshire, England. Lui sposato Unknown of Cheadle. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1225, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.Cheadle, Staffordshire, England: イギリススタフォードシュアイアチェアドル
ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Boscoは1175年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアチェアドルで生まれた。Unknown of Cheadleと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1225年に50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco(은)는 1175년 영국 스태퍼드실르 체아들일에서 태어났다. 그는 Unknown of Cheadle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1225년 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco nasceu em 1175, em Cheadle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Unknown of Cheadle. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1225, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco родился в 1175 г. в местности чидл, стаффордшир, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Unknown of Cheadle. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1225 г. в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Richard de Bosco nació en 1175, en Cheadle, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Unknown of Cheadle. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1225, a la edad de 50 años.
